artifacts.summary: Corrects task premise: ~/Mail/specs/ is a legitimate independent repo (218/219 files git-tracked, dating to Feb 2026), not a stray dump. Identifies the one genuine stray (empty untracked 072_email_workflow_infrastructure_prereqs/manifests/ stub) and its exact root cause: modules/home/email/agent-tools/lib.nix:35 hardcodes a repo-relative manifestDirDefault. Provides notmuch new.ignore guard and lib.nix absolute-path fix recommendations.

memory_candidates.content: ~/Mail and ~/.config/nvim are independent, long-lived git repos each running their own deployment of the Claude Code agent-system with their own specs/ task-numbering (distinct from ~/.dotfiles task numbers). When investigating filesystem artifacts under ~/Mail/specs/ or similar, check git log/ls-files/status on that repo first before assuming content is stray -- it is very likely legitimate, git-tracked history belonging to that sibling repo.

modules/home/email/agent-tools/lib.nix hardcodes manifestDirDefault as a repo-relative path (specs/072_email_workflow_infrastructure_prereqs/manifests). All five email wrapper binaries fall back to this when EMAIL_MANIFEST_DIR/--manifest-dir is not set, and mkdir -p it relative to the caller's cwd -- silently recreating stray directory trees anywhere the wrapper is invoked from an unexpected cwd (including once inside a live Gmail maildir folder). Fix: make the default an absolute, XDG-state-style path decoupled from any specific (archivable/renumberable) specs/ task directory.

notmuch new.ignore accepts two entry forms: bare filename/dirname (matched at any depth, regardless of location) or anchored regex delimited by slashes matched against the path relative to database.path (must use explicit caret/dollar anchors). When database.path spans a whole repo (like ~/Mail with database.path=~/Mail), use anchored regex entries for top-level non-mail directories to avoid noisy 'Ignoring non-mail file' logs on every notmuch new.
